mission.title: Install and Configure Alertmanager (Prometheus) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Alertmanager is a key component of the Prometheus ecosystem that handles alerts sent by client applications. It manages alert deduplication, grouping, and routing to various notification channels, making it essential for effective alert management in your observability stack.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Prometheus community Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the Alertmanager chart.
```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Alertmanager using Helm
steps.description:
Install Alertmanager with a specific version and create a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install alertmanager prometheus-community/alertmanager --version 0.31.1 --namespace monitoring --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Alertmanager pod is running successfully in the monitoring namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monitoring
```

steps.title: Configure Alertmanager
steps.description:
Create a configuration file for Alertmanager and apply it. Replace `<your_alertmanager_config.yml>` with your actual configuration file path.
```bash
kubectl create configmap alertmanager-config --from-file=<your_alertmanager_config.yml> --namespace monitoring
kubectl patch deployment alertmanager --namespace monitoring --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args", "value": ["--config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml"]}]'
```

steps.title: Expose Alertmanager service
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access Alertmanager from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/alertmanager --namespace monitoring 9093:9093
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Alertmanager pod running in the monitoring namespace and allow you to access the Alertmanager UI at http://localhost:9093. You should also have a working configuration applied to manage alerts.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

kubectl create configmap alertmanager-config --from-file=<your_alertmanager_config.yml> --namespace monitoring
kubectl patch deployment alertmanager --namespace monitoring --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args", "value": ["--config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml"]}]'

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Alertmanager release from the monitoring namespace using Helm to remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall alertmanager --namespace monitoring
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volumes that were created during the installation. This step ensures that no leftover resources remain.
```bash
kubectl delete crd alertmanagers.monitoring.coreos.com
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace monitoring
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the monitoring namespace to clean up all remaining resources and verify that it has been removed.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace monitoring
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current Alertmanager configuration and any persistent data. This ensures you can restore to the previous state if needed.
```bash
kubectl get configmap alertmanager-config --namespace monitoring -o yaml > alertmanager-config-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Alertmanager to the desired version. Replace `<new_version>` with the version you wish to upgrade to.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade alertmanager prometheus-community/alertmanager --version <new_version> --namespace monitoring
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Alertmanager pod to ensure it is running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monitoring
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This indicates that the Alertmanager pod is failing to start. Check the pod logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod_name> --namespace monitoring
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration errors
troubleshooting.description:
If Alertmanager fails to start due to configuration issues, validate the configuration file. You can check the configmap for any misconfigurations.
```bash
kubectl describe configmap alertmanager-config --namespace monitoring
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access Alertmanager, ensure the service is running and the port forwarding is set up correctly. Check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace monitoring
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the Alertmanager pod is not starting due to resource constraints, check the resource requests and limits in the deployment. Adjust them if necessary.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment alertmanager --namespace monitoring
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
